question: Is there an Indian restaurant near the Grand Canyon?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar in Ash Fork, AZ is about 78 miles from the Grand Canyon South Rim — roughly 1 hour 20 minutes via AZ-64 and I-40. Take I-40 Exit 146 to reach us.

question: Is Spice Grill & Bar a good I-40 food stop in Arizona?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar sits right off I-40 Exit 146 in Ash Fork, AZ — a quick pull-off for fresh Indian food. We serve full meals, takeout, and curbside so you can get back on the road fast.

question: Where is Spice Grill & Bar located?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar is at 33 Lewis Ave, Ash Fork, AZ 86320, right on I-40 Exit 146 and historic Route 66 — an easy stop for Grand Canyon visitors and I-40 travelers.

question: How far is Spice Grill & Bar from the Grand Canyon?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar is about 78 miles from the Grand Canyon South Rim, roughly 1 hour 20 minutes. Head south on AZ-64 to I-40 West, then take Exit 146 in Ash Fork.

question: Where to eat between Las Vegas and the Grand Canyon?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar in Ash Fork, AZ is a great stop along the route — about 3 hours from Las Vegas on I-40. Take Exit 146 for fresh Indian food on historic Route 66.

question: Where to eat between Flagstaff and the Grand Canyon?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar is about 51 miles west of Flagstaff on I-40, roughly a 46-minute drive. Take Exit 146 in Ash Fork for Indian food before or after your Grand Canyon visit.

question: Where to eat on the way from Phoenix to the Grand Canyon?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar in Ash Fork, AZ sits about 3 hours north of Phoenix via I-17 to I-40. Take Exit 146 for a flavorful stop before heading up to the Grand Canyon.

question: Where to eat between Kingman and the Grand Canyon?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar is about 97 miles east of Kingman on I-40 — about 1.5 hours. Take Exit 146 in Ash Fork for Indian food, roughly midway between Kingman and the Grand Canyon turnoff.

question: Where to eat between Los Angeles and the Grand Canyon?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar in Ash Fork, AZ is about 6 hours east of Los Angeles via I-15 and I-40. Take Exit 146 for fresh Indian food on historic Route 66 — a flavorful stop before the final stretch to the Grand Canyon.

question: What type of food does Spice Grill & Bar serve?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar serves Punjabi Indian cuisine — Tandoori dishes, rich curries, and freshly baked naan. We specialize in Butter Chicken, Goat Curry, Shahi Paneer, and tandoor-fired breads.

question: What are the most popular dishes at Spice Grill & Bar?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar's guest favorites are Butter Chicken, Goat Curry, Shahi Paneer, Fish Pakora, and our signature Garlic Naan baked in a traditional clay oven.

question: What should I order if I've never had Indian food before?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar recommends Butter Chicken for first-timers — it's mild, creamy, and crowd-pleasing. Pair it with Garlic Naan and try Tandoori Chicken for a well-rounded introduction.

question: Can I customize how spicy my food is at Spice Grill & Bar?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar can adjust dishes from mild to extra hot. Just let your server know your heat preference — we're happy to dial it back for travelers new to Indian cuisine.

question: Does Spice Grill & Bar have vegetarian or vegan options?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar offers a wide variety of vegetarian and vegan-friendly dishes, including Shahi Paneer, Dal Tadka, Aloo Gobhi, and Chana Masala — all prepared with freshly ground spices.

question: What are the operating hours for Spice Grill & Bar?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar is open Monday through Thursday 8am–9pm, Friday through Sunday 8am–10pm.

question: Do you offer takeout or delivery services?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar offers takeout, pickup, and curbside service. You can place your order online through our secure ordering partner, Toast.

question: Can I order ahead from Williams or Seligman?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar offers call-ahead pickup — call us at 928-277-1292, place your order, and your food will be ready when you arrive at the restaurant in Ash Fork. We do not deliver.

question: Is there parking available at Spice Grill & Bar?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar has car and motorcycle parking at the restaurant. RV and truck parking is available across the road at the truck stop — convenient for big rigs and road trip setups.

question: Is Spice Grill & Bar biker-friendly?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar is a biker-friendly stop on historic Route 66, with motorcycle parking at the restaurant. We welcome riders cruising the Mother Road through Ash Fork.

question: Does Spice Grill & Bar serve alcohol?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar includes a full bar service with a wide variety of beers, cocktails, wines, and other alcoholic beverages to complement your meal.

question: Is Spice Grill & Bar kid-friendly or family-friendly?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar is a family-friendly restaurant with a welcoming atmosphere and seating for small and large groups, including families with children.

question: How far is Spice Grill & Bar from Williams, AZ?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar is about 18 miles west of Williams, AZ on I-40 — roughly 18 minutes. Take Exit 146 in Ash Fork. We are the closest Indian restaurant to Williams along the I-40 corridor.

question: Is there an Indian restaurant near Kaibab Estates West?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar in Ash Fork is about 5 miles south of Kaibab Estates West — roughly a 6-minute drive. Take the local road from Kaibab to Ash Fork, then I-40 Exit 146 to reach us.

question: What payment methods does Spice Grill & Bar accept?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar accepts cash, all major credit cards (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover), debit cards, Apple Pay, and Google Pay. No minimum purchase for card transactions.

question: Do I need a reservation at Spice Grill & Bar?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar is walk-in only — no reservations needed. Just stop by during our open hours: Monday through Thursday 8am to 9pm, Friday through Sunday 8am to 10pm.

question: Is Spice Grill & Bar budget-friendly?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar is moderately priced — most curries fall between $14.99 and $18.99 and naan between $2.99 and $7.99. Family-style sharing makes it an affordable stop on Route 66.

question: Does Spice Grill & Bar deliver?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar does not offer in-house delivery. Order takeout or curbside via Toast at order.toasttab.com or call (928) 277-1292. Third-party delivery apps are not currently integrated.

question: Does Spice Grill & Bar offer takeout?
answer: Yes. Spice Grill & Bar offers takeout, pickup, and curbside service. Order online through Toast at order.toasttab.com or call (928) 277-1292 to place your order ahead.

question: What's the best Indian restaurant on I-40 in Arizona?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar in Ash Fork — at I-40 Exit 146 — is the only authentic Punjabi restaurant on this stretch of I-40 between Flagstaff and Kingman. Tandoor cooking, fresh naan, and full bar.

question: Is Spice Grill & Bar known for Butter Chicken?
answer: Yes. Spice Grill & Bar's Butter Chicken is a signature dish — chicken thighs simmered in rich butter-cream and tomato sauce. Pair with Garlic Naan. A reliable first order for travelers new to Indian cuisine.

question: Does Spice Grill & Bar have Tandoori dishes?
answer: Yes. Spice Grill & Bar runs a traditional clay-oven tandoor. Tandoori Chicken (8 pieces) and Paneer Tikka are served as appetizers; Tandoori Wings and tandoor-baked naan round out the menu.

question: Can I order mild Indian food at Spice Grill & Bar?
answer: Yes. Spice Grill & Bar prepares dishes mild on request — Butter Chicken, Shahi Paneer, and Korma curries are naturally mild. Tell your server your spice preference and we will dial it back accordingly.

question: Can Spice Grill & Bar accommodate large groups?
answer: Yes. Spice Grill & Bar seats large groups in our family-friendly dining room. For parties of six or more, calling (928) 277-1292 ahead helps us prepare. Family-style sharing pairs well with our menu.

question: Is Spice Grill & Bar a good place to stop while driving Route 66?
answer: Spice Grill & Bar sits directly on historic Route 66 in Ash Fork at I-40 Exit 146. RV and truck parking is across the road; bikers and road-trippers are welcome. A genuine roadside stop, not a chain.
